message:
Add RFC 6587 octet-counting support to parse_syslog

The `parse_syslog` function now supports RFC 6587 octet-counted framing
via the new `octet_counting` parameter:

- Not specified: auto-detect and strip length prefix if valid
- `octet_counting=true`: require prefix, warn if missing
- `octet_counting=false`: never strip prefix

The implementation introduces `octet_length_parser`, a shared parser
combinator used by both `parse_syslog` (single-value) and `read_syslog`
(streaming) to ensure consistent behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+---
+title: RFC 6587 octet-counting support for syslog parsing
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2026-01-21T07:41:37.394369Z
+---
+
+The `parse_syslog` function and `read_syslog` operator now support RFC 6587 octet-counted framing, where syslog messages are prefixed with their byte length (for example, `65 <syslog-message>`). This framing is commonly used in TCP-based syslog transport to handle message boundaries.
+
+The new `octet_counting` parameter offers three modes:
+
+- **Not specified (default)**: Auto-detect. The parser strips a length prefix if present and valid, otherwise parses the input as-is. This prevents false positives where input coincidentally starts with digits and a space.
+- **`octet_counting=true`**: Require a length prefix. Emits a warning and returns null if the input lacks a valid prefix.
+- **`octet_counting=false`**: Never strip a length prefix. Parse the input as-is.
+
+The implementation uses a shared `octet_length_parser` combinator across both streaming (`read_syslog`) and single-value (`parse_syslog`) parsing paths, ensuring consistent behavior.

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+// Test octet counting in parse_syslog
+// - Auto-detect with octet-counted input (65 bytes after the space)
+// - Auto-detect with regular syslog (no prefix)
+from { x: "65 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" },
+     { x: "<165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog()

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Test explicit octet_counting=false (never strips prefix)
+from { x: "<165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=false)

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Test explicit octet_counting=true (expects prefix, warns if not found)
+from { x: "65 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Test octet_counting=true with input missing the octet prefix (should warn)
+from { x: "<165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+{
+  x: null,
+}
+warning: expected octet-counted input
+ --> tests/functions/parse_syslog/octet_counting_missing.tql:3:5
+  |
+3 | x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)
+  |     ~ 
+  |
